tone_guard: Keep the tone calm, professional, and publication-ready.
Do not sound alarmist, robotic, or salesy.
Prefer clear editorial language over generic AI phrasing.
do_not_invent: Do not add new facts, statistics, dates, or named entities that are not already supported by the source context.
Do not fabricate citations, sources, credentials, or claims of authority.
must_preserve: Preserve factual meaning, topical scope, and article intent.
Preserve named entities, numbers, and dates already present in the target text.
scope_guard: Edit only the resolved repair scope.
Do not rewrite unrelated blocks, headings, or later sections.

must_change: Shorten the opening answer without removing key meaning.
Keep the first answer directly quotable and easy to reuse.
Move the direct answer closer to the opening of the target section.
Reduce setup before the answer appears.
Make the first answer directly match the question being asked.
Remove indirect lead-in phrasing that delays the answer.

must_preserve: Keep the heading text and section promise intact.
must_change: Strengthen the supporting text under the heading.
Add concrete support that fulfills the heading promise.
Only restructure into a list when it materially improves scanability.
Convert dense comparison prose into a clearer list only when it improves readability.
Separate reusable question-answer pairs more clearly if the section is still dense inline prose.
Simplify sentence shape and reduce avoidable reading friction.
Keep the original meaning intact while improving scanability.
Keep claims within what the current text can responsibly support.
Rewrite weak or ambiguous factual phrasing into clear statements.
Keep the claim precise without overstating certainty.
Make each step explicit, ordered, and action-led.
Keep the sequence easy to extract as instructions.
